assets.title: Big Five Personality
assets.summary: The Big Five describes personality differences through five continuous dimensions: openness, conscientiousness, extraversion, agreeableness, and neuroticism.

seo.title: Big Five Personality | FermatMind Big Five
seo.description: The Big Five describes personality differences through five continuous dimensions: openness, conscientiousness, extraversion, agreeableness, and neuroticis

faq.question: Is Big Five a type test?
faq.answer: No. Big Five is dimensional and does not place people into a small set of fixed types.

method_boundary.summary: Big Five is a dimensional model, not a clinical diagnosis, hiring screen, or fixed type label.

evidence_notes.note: Product copy should use bounded wording such as tends to, may, and often.

sections.title: Model overview
sections.body_md: The Big Five describes personality differences through five continuous dimensions: openness, conscientiousness, extraversion, agreeableness, and neuroticism.

sections.title: Five dimensions
sections.body_md: Each dimension is continuous. High and low poles can both be useful in different contexts.

sections.title: Appropriate uses
sections.body_md: Use it for self-understanding, communication preferences, and reflection, not diagnosis or deterministic decisions.

sections.title: Tendency overview
sections.body_md: High openness often shows up as curiosity, comfort with ambiguity, creative exploration, and interest in ideas beyond routine practice.

sections.title: Concrete example
sections.body_md: A high-openness person may enjoy learning unfamiliar frameworks, changing a workflow after evidence appears, or turning abstract ideas into experiments.

sections.title: Balancing note
sections.body_md: This page explains one pole only; a real profile should combine all five domains and lived context.
